item.title: Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) Epidemiology Fellowship 2026–2028 (Fully Funded) | Apply by 26 August 2026
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) – Epidemiology Track 2026–2028, a fully funded two-year fellowship for young public health professionals from African Union Member States. The programme includes three months of in-person training in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, followed by 21 months of field-based applied epidemiology training, with travel costs, […]

The post Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) Epidemiology Fellowship 2026–2028 (Fully Funded) | Apply by 26 August 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) – Epidemiology Track 2026–2028, a fully funded two-year fellowship for young public health professionals from African Union Member States. The programme includes three months of in-person training in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, followed by 21 months of field-based applied epidemiology training, with travel costs, monthly stipends, health insurance, accommodation-related support, learning materials, hardware, software, and mentorship fully covered. The fellowship begins in October 2026, and applications close on 26 August 2026.

Fellowship at a Glance

  • Host: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC)
  • Programme: African Epidemic Services (AES) – Epidemiology Track
  • Duration: 2 years (24 months)
  • Funding: Fully funded
  • Training Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia (first 3 months)
  • Field Placement: 21 months in an African Union Member State
  • Eligible Countries: Citizens of African Union Member States
  • Age Requirement: Under 35 years
  • Application Deadline: 26 August 2026
  • Programme Starts: October 2026

About Africa CDC

The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention is an autonomous technical Agency established by the African Union (AU) to enhance the capacity, capabilities, and partnerships of Africa’s public health institutions.

Africa CDC is mandated through Article 3 of its governing statute to “support Member States in capacity building in public health through medium and long-term field epidemiologic and laboratory training programmes”. To fulfil this mandate, Africa CDC convened the Africa Health Workforce Compact in 2025, with a focus on investment, training, and retaining of skilled health workforce. The Compact builds upon the Africa CDC Framework for Health Workforce Development 2020–2025 and supports the African Union Agenda 2063 through the development of a sufficient, motivated, and well-trained health workforce capable of delivering essential health services across the continent.

About the Fellowship

Africa CDC has developed a continental African Epidemic Service (AES) Program, a flagship training program aiming to enhance the capacity, capabilities, and partnerships of Africa’s public health institutions. Its primary goal is to build capacity of mid-level professionals to control, prevent, detect, and respond to disease threats and outbreaks by employing science, policy, and data-driven interventions and programs effectively and efficiently whilst learning from best practices across the continent. The African Epidemic Service has three tracks namely, Epidemiology, Laboratory Leadership Fellowship and Public health informatics.

The African Epidemic Services – Epidemiology track is a full time two-year competency based applied epidemiology training program with three months in-person didactic training in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, and 21 months of applied training in a site hosted by an African Union Member State. The program aims to strengthen the disease prevention and response capacity of African Union Member States. Successful applicants will participate in experiential Core Activities of Learning (CALs) that include, but are not limited to, data analysis, surveillance system evaluation, and outbreak investigation. The AES-Epidemiology fellow will have the support of an expert supervisor and or a mentor who will provide support throughout the fellowship programme.

Africa CDC will provide all learning and development materials and meet cover costs associated with the fellowship, including travel-related costs, stipends, hardware, software and health insurance.

Education

Applicants for the Fellowship must:

  1. Be a citizen of an African Union Member State.
  2. Be a youth as defined by the African Union, i.e., an individual under the age of 35.
  3. At the time of application, be employed in an African country with permission from employing government institution to attend the two years training, with guarantee to return to employment post training;
  4. Have a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree from an accredited university in the one health or related fields such as human medicine, nursing veterinary medicine, environmental sciences, public health or related fields or other doctoral-level training;
  5. Demonstrate satisfactory completion of coursework in epidemiology or quantitative research methods; and biostatistics
  6. Have at least three years of recent full-time public health post graduate experience in an African country.
  7. Demonstrate ability to read and prepare technical medical and epidemiological communication materials such as scientific publications, scientific conference presentations etc
  8. Proficiency in at least one of the African Union’s official languages (English, French, Arabic, Portuguese, Spanish, or Kiswahili) is required. Knowledge of an additional AU official language is an asset

Note: Advanced FETP Fellows and Graduates are not eligible to apply.

CLICK HERE TO APPLY OR GET MORE DETAILS 

Requirements

All applications must be submitted using the online application form. Incomplete applications will not be considered. All applicants must provide the following information:

  1. A national passport from an African Union Member State.
  2. An official letter of support with permission from a government institution to attend the two-year training programme with the right to return post training.
  3. Letters of recommendation from two reputable professional referees.
  4. An official copy of degree certificate and transcripts of your bachelor’s or master’s degree.
  5. Brief curriculum vitae using the AU template.
  6. Motivation statement (maximum 500 words), outlining why this program would be a good fit, and further outlining how this training will impact your country.
  7. Optional: Evidence of additional relevant qualifications.

Important Information

Completed applications with all supporting documents should be uploaded to the online portal. Incomplete applications will not be considered. The fellowship program will prioritize inclusivity, gender equity, and representation from across the continent.

Key Dates

  • Application Deadline: 26 August 2026
  • Programme Start Date: October 2026

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete the online application form and upload all required supporting documents before the deadline. Incomplete applications will not be considered.

The fellowship promotes gender equality, inclusivity, and broad representation across African Union Member States.

CLICK HERE TO APPLY OR GET MORE DETAILS 

The Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) Epidemiology Fellowship 2026–2028 is an outstanding opportunity for young public health professionals to receive world-class epidemiology training while helping strengthen disease surveillance and outbreak response systems across Africa.

Application Deadline: 26 August 2026

The post Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) Epidemiology Fellowship 2026–2028 (Fully Funded) | Apply by 26 August 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Oslo Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026 in Children, Youth and Digital Media: Fully Funded Four-Year Research Opportunity in Norway
item.dc:creator: Fridah Kamuri
item.description:
The University of Oslo (UiO) is inviting applications for its prestigious Postdoctoral Research Fellowship in Children, Youth and Digital Media under the internationally recognized EU Kids Online Norway project. This outstanding research opportunity is designed for highly motivated early-career researchers who are passionate about studying the impact of digital technologies on children and young people. […]

The post University of Oslo Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026 in Children, Youth and Digital Media: Fully Funded Four-Year Research Opportunity in Norway appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Oslo (UiO) is inviting applications for its prestigious Postdoctoral Research Fellowship in Children, Youth and Digital Media under the internationally recognized EU Kids Online Norway project. This outstanding research opportunity is designed for highly motivated early-career researchers who are passionate about studying the impact of digital technologies on children and young people.

Hosted by the Department of Media and Communication, this four-year postdoctoral fellowship offers scholars an opportunity to conduct independent, high-impact research while collaborating with one of Europe’s leading interdisciplinary research networks focused on children’s digital lives. Researchers will join the Centre for Studies of Children, Youth, and Media and contribute to internationally significant studies examining how digital media shapes children’s experiences, opportunities, rights, wellbeing, and online safety.

If you are looking to advance your academic career while contributing to globally relevant research on digital media and youth, this fellowship presents an exceptional opportunity.

About the University of Oslo

The University of Oslo (UiO) is Norway’s oldest and highest-ranked university. With approximately 26,500 students and over 7,200 employees, UiO is internationally recognized for excellence in education, research, and innovation.

Its Department of Media and Communication is one of Scandinavia’s leading academic environments dedicated to research on media, communication, politics, journalism, digital technologies, and society. Through interdisciplinary collaboration, the department contributes to global debates on communication and digital transformation.

The successful fellow will become part of this vibrant academic community while collaborating closely with researchers across Europe through the EU Kids Online network.

About the Fellowship

The Postdoctoral Research Fellowship is attached to the EU Kids Online Norway project.

EU Kids Online is one of Europe’s most influential research networks studying children’s digital experiences. It brings together researchers from numerous countries to investigate:

  • Children’s internet use
  • Online opportunities
  • Digital wellbeing
  • Online risks
  • Digital rights
  • Digital literacy
  • Media regulation
  • Digital skills
  • Technology and youth development

The selected fellow will develop an independent research project that aligns with the objectives of EU Kids Online Norway while contributing new scientific knowledge to this rapidly evolving field.

Research Areas

Applicants are encouraged to propose innovative research within the broad field of children’s and young people’s digital lives.

Relevant topics include, but are not limited to:

  • Children’s digital media use
  • Social media and young people
  • Digital wellbeing
  • Online risks
  • Cyber safety
  • Children’s online rights
  • Media regulation
  • Digital literacy
  • Digital skills
  • Internet governance
  • Online opportunities
  • Digital inequalities
  • Technology and education
  • Youth media participation
  • Digital citizenship

Projects may focus on:

  • Individual-level factors
  • Family influences
  • Community-level influences
  • National policies
  • International comparisons
  • Cross-country research

Comparative and interdisciplinary studies are particularly welcomed.

Access to Valuable Research Data

One of the major advantages of this fellowship is access to extensive research resources.

Depending on ethical approval and data access procedures, the fellow may gain access to:

  • National EU Kids Online datasets
  • International comparative datasets
  • Existing research trajectories
  • Established European research collaborations

Using existing datasets is considered an advantage but is not mandatory. Researchers are free to propose original methodological approaches suitable for their research questions.

Fellowship Responsibilities

The selected researcher will undertake a range of academic responsibilities, including:

Conduct Independent Research

Develop and implement a high-quality research project that contributes original knowledge to the field.

Participate in Academic Activities

Contribute actively to:

  • Research seminars
  • Workshops
  • Academic discussions
  • Collaborative research
  • Centre activities

Research Dissemination

Expected dissemination activities include:

  • Publishing scientific articles
  • Presenting research findings
  • Participating in conferences
  • Supporting public engagement activities

Strengthen Research Networks

The fellow will contribute to expanding both national and international collaboration within the EU Kids Online network.

Teaching and Career Development

Unlike many research fellowships, this position includes structured career development.

The appointment lasts four years, including:

  • 75% research
  • 25% teaching, administration, and career development

The teaching component also allows the fellow to obtain formal teaching qualifications required for future academic positions.

A personalized career plan will also be developed to support long-term professional growth.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • A PhD or equivalent degree in:
    • Media and Communication
    • Sociology
    • Psychology
    • Education
    • Childhood Studies
    • Or another closely related discipline

Applicants whose doctoral thesis has been submitted for evaluation before the application deadline are also eligible, provided the dissertation is successfully defended before appointment.

Candidates must also demonstrate:

  • Excellent written English
  • Excellent spoken English
  • Strong motivation
  • Personal suitability

Preferred Qualifications

Although not mandatory, preference may be given to applicants with experience in:

  • Research on children and youth
  • Digital technologies
  • Internet studies
  • Qualitative research
  • Quantitative research
  • Mixed methods
  • Digital methods
  • International research collaboration
  • Interdisciplinary research
  • University teaching

Research Proposal Requirements

Applicants must submit a comprehensive research proposal.

The proposal should clearly describe:

  • Research questions
  • Objectives
  • Literature background
  • Theoretical framework
  • Research methodology
  • Data collection
  • Data analysis
  • Timeline
  • Progress plan

The proposal should be between 3 and 5 pages, with a maximum of 14,000 characters.

Evaluation Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on:

Scientific Excellence

  • Originality
  • Innovation
  • Academic contribution

Methodological Quality

  • Appropriate research methods
  • Feasibility
  • Research design

Relevance

Alignment with:

  • Centre for Studies of Children, Youth, and Media
  • EU Kids Online Norway

Personal Competencies

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Collaboration skills
  • Communication skills
  • Critical thinking
  • Time management
  • Self-motivation
  • Ability to contribute positively to the research environment

Salary and Benefits

Successful applicants will receive:

  • Annual salary between NOK 595,000 and NOK 677,000, depending on qualifications and experience
  • Membership in the Norwegian Public Service Pension Fund
  • Excellent insurance benefits
  • Attractive welfare benefits
  • Career development support
  • Access to world-class research facilities
  • Opportunities to collaborate internationally
  • Professional mentoring
  • A stimulating interdisciplinary research environment

Required Application Documents

Applicants should prepare:

  1. Motivation letter
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Research proposal
  4. List of publications
  5. Copies of academic certificates and diplomas (BA, MA, PhD)

All documents must be submitted in English or a Scandinavian language.

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship offers numerous advantages, including:

  • Four years of funded research
  • International collaboration opportunities
  • Participation in one of Europe’s leading research networks
  • Access to unique datasets
  • Teaching experience
  • Career development support
  • Competitive salary and benefits
  • Strong pathway toward an Associate Professor position
  • Work at Norway’s leading university
  • Opportunities to publish internationally

Visit HERE for more information about the University of Oslo Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026 in Children, Youth and Digital Media

Application Deadline

Deadline: 15 August 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the closing date to avoid last-minute technical issues.

APPLY HERE

Conclusion

The University of Oslo’s Postdoctoral Research Fellowship in Children, Youth and Digital Media is an exceptional opportunity for emerging researchers seeking to establish themselves as leaders in digital media and childhood studies. Through the internationally renowned EU Kids Online network, successful applicants will gain access to collaborative research, valuable datasets, career development opportunities, and one of Europe’s strongest academic environments. If your research interests focus on children’s digital experiences, online wellbeing, media regulation, or digital literacy, this fellowship provides an ideal platform to produce impactful research while advancing your academic career in Norway.

For more global Fellowship opportunities visit OFY HERE 

The post University of Oslo Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026 in Children, Youth and Digital Media: Fully Funded Four-Year Research Opportunity in Norway appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Entries: InvigoratEU Contest 2026 – Share Your Vision for the Future of Europe and Stand a Chance to Attend a Fully Funded Conference in Brussels
item.dc:creator: Nana Safo
item.description:
The InvigoratEU Contest 2026 is now accepting submissions from creative thinkers, writers, students, professionals, and individuals worldwide who have a vision for the future of Europe. Organized by the Trans European Policy Studies Association (TEPSA), this international contest provides participants with a unique opportunity to contribute meaningful ideas on the future of the European continent […]

The post Call for Entries: InvigoratEU Contest 2026 – Share Your Vision for the Future of Europe and Stand a Chance to Attend a Fully Funded Conference in Brussels appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The InvigoratEU Contest 2026 is now accepting submissions from creative thinkers, writers, students, professionals, and individuals worldwide who have a vision for the future of Europe. Organized by the Trans European Policy Studies Association (TEPSA), this international contest provides participants with a unique opportunity to contribute meaningful ideas on the future of the European continent while gaining international recognition.

As Europe continues to face major geopolitical, economic, and security challenges, discussions surrounding the future of the European Union (EU) have become increasingly important. Through this contest, TEPSA aims to encourage public participation by inviting people from around the world to express their ideas through essays, poetry, or audiovisual storytelling.

Successful participants will have the opportunity to showcase their work before leading European researchers, policymakers, and experts, while three winners will receive invitations to attend the InvigoratEU Final Conference in Brussels with travel and accommodation support.

About the InvigoratEU Contest

The European Union is entering a defining period in its history. The possibility of EU enlargement has become one of the continent’s most significant policy discussions, especially following Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, which exposed vulnerabilities across Europe.

Issues such as:

  • Energy dependence
  • Supply chain disruptions
  • Security challenges
  • Economic resilience
  • Regional cooperation
  • Defence collaboration

have highlighted the importance of stronger European integration.

While policymakers increasingly view enlargement as an investment in Europe’s long-term resilience, many citizens remain uncertain about its benefits. Misunderstandings and misinformation have contributed to growing scepticism regarding the expansion of the European Union.

The InvigoratEU Contest seeks to bridge this gap by encouraging citizens and residents from around the world to share their own perspectives on Europe’s future.

Rather than limiting the conversation to policymakers, academics, and institutions, TEPSA wants ordinary people to contribute innovative ideas that could help shape Europe’s future.

Contest Theme

Participants are invited to explore the following central theme:

“What future for the European continent?”

Submissions should focus on Europe’s future, European Union enlargement, and the opportunities that a wider European Union could create for its member states and citizens.

Submission Categories

Applicants may participate by submitting work in one of the following three categories.

1. Written Essays

Applicants may submit an original essay of no more than 2,000 words.

Essays should answer one of the following questions:

  • How can a wider European Union contribute to Europe’s security and resilience?
  • How can EU enlargement help build a more competitive and self-sufficient European continent?
  • How can enlargement create tangible benefits for everyday citizens in both current and future Member States?

Essays should demonstrate originality, thoughtful analysis, and a clear understanding of the topic.

2. Poetry

Participants may submit an original poem of up to 500 words.

The poem should creatively explore one or more of the following themes:

  • Europe’s future
  • European security
  • Economic competitiveness
  • EU enlargement
  • Benefits for European citizens

Poems should reflect the author’s unique perspective and creative expression.

3. Audiovisual Works

Applicants may also submit an original audiovisual production lasting no longer than two minutes.

Accepted formats include:

  • Interviews
  • Vlogs
  • Documentary-style videos
  • Animations
  • Short films
  • Creative visual storytelling
  • Any other original video format

Participants may:

  • Appear on camera themselves
  • Feature other people
  • Use narration only
  • Create animated content
  • Use visual storytelling without appearing in the video

Videos may be recorded using any available equipment and should be submitted either as a video file or through a downloadable link.

Who Can Apply?

The competition welcomes participants from across the globe.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Students
  • Young professionals
  • Individuals interested in European affairs
  • Writers
  • Artists
  • Poets
  • Content creators
  • Researchers
  • Members of the public interested in EU enlargement

Applicants must:

  • Be at least 18 years old.
  • Be able to submit original work.
  • Meet the submission requirements.

There are no nationality or residency restrictions, making this a truly global opportunity.

Benefits of Participating

Selected winners will receive valuable international exposure and networking opportunities.

Benefits include:

  • Invitation to attend the InvigoratEU Final Conference.
  • Conference dates: 4–5 November 2026.
  • Venue: Brussels, Belgium.
  • Travel and accommodation support of up to €500.
  • Opportunity to present work before leading researchers, policymakers, and practitioners.
  • Recognition by the Trans European Policy Studies Association (TEPSA).
  • Online promotion of winning submissions through TEPSA’s communication platforms.
  • Increased visibility within European policy and academic networks.

How to Apply

Applicants should prepare their original submission in their chosen category and send it via email.

Submission Email

communications@tepsa.eu

Subject Line

InvigoratEU public contest_LAST NAME

Replace “LAST NAME” with your surname before submitting.

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting entries is:

24 July 2026

Late submissions may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to submit well before the deadline.

For more global opportunities visit OFY Website HERE

Important Tips Before You Submit

To improve your chances of success:

  • Submit only original work.
  • Carefully follow the word and time limits.
  • Address the contest theme clearly.
  • Ensure your submission reflects your own ideas and creativity.
  • Proofread your work before submission.
  • Use a clear and professional file name.
  • Submit before the deadline to avoid technical issues.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The InvigoratEU Contest offers much more than a traditional writing or creative competition. It provides a platform for people around the world to participate in one of Europe’s most important conversations about its future.

Whether you are passionate about politics, international relations, security, economics, creative writing, filmmaking, or public policy, this contest allows you to contribute meaningful ideas while gaining international recognition.

For students and young professionals especially, participating can strengthen academic and professional portfolios, expand international networks, and provide valuable exposure within European policy circles.

For more information, visit TEPSA website HERE

Conclusion

The InvigoratEU Contest 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for individuals worldwide to share innovative ideas about the future of Europe through essays, poetry, or audiovisual storytelling. Beyond the chance to win a fully supported trip to Brussels, participants can have their work showcased before influential policymakers, researchers, and practitioners working on the future of European integration. If you are passionate about Europe’s future and have a creative vision to share, this is an opportunity worth exploring. Ensure your submission is original, follows all competition guidelines, and reaches the organizers no later than 24 July 2026.

The post Call for Entries: InvigoratEU Contest 2026 – Share Your Vision for the Future of Europe and Stand a Chance to Attend a Fully Funded Conference in Brussels appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Pathways to Scale Program 2026: Exclusive Mastercard Foundation & WUSC Business Support for Women-Led Businesses
item.dc:creator: Ning Ma
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Pathways to Scale Program 2026. Women entrepreneurs across Africa continue to play a critical role in driving economic growth, creating jobs, and transforming rural and peri-urban communities. To help women-led enterprises overcome barriers to finance, business development, and market access, the Pathways to Scale (P2S) Program is accepting applications […]

The post Pathways to Scale Program 2026: Exclusive Mastercard Foundation & WUSC Business Support for Women-Led Businesses appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Pathways to Scale Program 2026.
Women entrepreneurs across Africa continue to play a critical role in driving economic growth, creating jobs, and transforming rural and peri-urban communities. To help women-led enterprises overcome barriers to finance, business development, and market access, the Pathways to Scale (P2S) Program is accepting applications from eligible businesses operating in Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda.

Implemented by World University Service of Canada (WUSC) in partnership with the Mastercard Foundation, the Pathways to Scale Program is a major initiative designed to strengthen women-owned and women-led businesses through tailored business support, access to finance, expert mentorship, and strategic networking opportunities.

Applications for the current intake are open until 31 July 2026.

About the Pathways to Scale (P2S) Program

Pathways to Scale (P2S) is a multi-country initiative that aims to help women entrepreneurs expand their businesses while increasing employment opportunities for young women across Africa. The program focuses on enterprises that have demonstrated growth potential and are positioned to scale their operations with the right combination of financial and technical support.

The initiative is implemented by World University Service of Canada (WUSC) with support from the Mastercard Foundation. According to the program, P2S seeks to strengthen women-led enterprises by improving access to financing, strengthening business systems, and creating stronger connections with investors, financial institutions, and corporate partners.

The broader program is expected to support thousands of women entrepreneurs while contributing to sustainable economic development across participating countries.

Benefits of the Pathways to Scale Program

Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive package of business support designed to accelerate enterprise growth.

Benefits include:

  • Access to financing opportunities
  • Tailored business support powered by SIGMA™ AI
  • Individualized mentorship from experienced business and industry experts
  • Advisory support to strengthen business performance
  • Opportunities to connect with investors
  • Connections with corporate partners
  • Linkages to financial institutions
  • Networking opportunities within a strong ecosystem of entrepreneurs and development partners

Rather than offering only financial support, the program combines funding pathways with practical business development services that help enterprises become more competitive and sustainable.

Who Can Apply?

The Pathways to Scale Program 2026 is designed for established women-owned and women-led businesses that are ready to scale their operations and increase their impact. The current application round targets enterprises operating in selected sectors across four African countries.

To be eligible for the Pathways to Scale Program 2026, applicants should meet the following requirements:

Business Ownership

Applicants must:

  • Own or manage a women-owned or women-led business.

Eligible Countries

The business must be legally operating in one of the following countries:

  • Ethiopia
  • Ghana
  • Nigeria
  • Rwanda

Eligible Sectors

Businesses should operate in:

  • Agriculture
  • Manufacturing
  • Related sectors

Business Requirements

Applicants should also:

  • Demonstrate measurable impact in rural or peri-urban communities.
  • Generate annual revenues of more than US$650,000.
  • Be seeking opportunities to grow their business through access to finance, tailored business support, mentorship, and strategic partnerships.

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official eligibility criteria and complete the online application form with accurate and up-to-date business information before submitting their application.

Program Focus

The Pathways to Scale Program seeks businesses capable of creating long-term economic impact.

Priority is given to enterprises that:

  • Create employment opportunities.
  • Strengthen local value chains.
  • Support rural and peri-urban economic development.
  • Demonstrate business growth potential.
  • Contribute to sustainable and inclusive economic development.

The program recognizes that women entrepreneurs often face greater challenges in accessing finance and investment. Through mentorship, advisory services, and investor engagement, P2S aims to reduce these barriers and enable businesses to scale successfully.

Why Apply?

Participating businesses gain access to more than funding alone.

Successful enterprises will benefit from:

  • Business growth support tailored to their needs.
  • AI-powered business advisory tools through SIGMA™ AI.
  • Guidance from experienced mentors.
  • Increased visibility among investors and financial institutions.
  • Expanded professional networks.
  • Opportunities to strengthen operational systems and long-term sustainability.

For businesses seeking to expand operations, improve management systems, or secure future investment, the program offers a valuable platform for growth.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official online application form.

During the application process, applicants will be asked to:

  1. Confirm whether they are completing the application themselves or on behalf of another participant.
  2. Read and acknowledge the Data Privacy and Consent Notice.
  3. Agree to the consent terms for data sharing.
  4. Complete the eligibility and due diligence questions.
  5. Submit the required business information through the online application portal.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare accurate business information before beginning the application.

APPLY HERE

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: 31 July 2026

Late applications may not be considered.

Conclusion

The Pathways to Scale (P2S) Program represents an important opportunity for established women-led businesses seeking to expand their impact across Africa. By combining access to finance with mentorship, AI-powered business support, investor connections, and strategic networking, the program provides a comprehensive growth platform for enterprises operating in Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda.

Women entrepreneurs who meet the eligibility requirements are encouraged to submit their applications before the 31 July 2026 deadline and take advantage of this opportunity to strengthen their businesses and contribute to sustainable economic development.

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more global job opportunities, click here.

The post Pathways to Scale Program 2026: Exclusive Mastercard Foundation & WUSC Business Support for Women-Led Businesses appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Earth Journalism Network (EJN) Media Grants 2026–2027: Receive Up to US$10,000 to Strengthen Marine Conservation Journalism
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Earth Journalism Network (EJN), an initiative of Internews, has officially opened applications for its 2026–2027 Media Grants aimed at strengthening journalism on marine conservation and countries’ commitments toward achieving the global 30×30 biodiversity target. The programme offers five media grants of up to US$10,000 each to eligible newsrooms and media organizations that are committed […]

The post Earth Journalism Network (EJN) Media Grants 2026–2027: Receive Up to US$10,000 to Strengthen Marine Conservation Journalism appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Earth Journalism Network (EJN), an initiative of Internews, has officially opened applications for its 2026–2027 Media Grants aimed at strengthening journalism on marine conservation and countries’ commitments toward achieving the global 30×30 biodiversity target.

The programme offers five media grants of up to US$10,000 each to eligible newsrooms and media organizations that are committed to producing impactful reporting projects, building journalists’ capacity, and increasing public awareness of marine conservation issues.

Applications are open to media organizations located in eligible coastal countries worldwide, with special priority given to applicants from Ghana, Mexico, and the Philippines.

Application Deadline: 10 August 2026 (11:59 PM Asia/Manila).

About the Earth Journalism Network Media Grants

Marine ecosystems play a critical role in maintaining biodiversity, supporting livelihoods, regulating climate, and sustaining millions of people around the world. Despite growing international commitments to protect the world’s oceans, progress remains slow.

In December 2022, over 190 countries adopted the Global Biodiversity Framework, committing to protect and effectively manage 30% of the world’s terrestrial, inland water, coastal, and marine areas by 2030—commonly known as the 30×30 Target.

However, global assessments show that only around 3.5% of the world’s oceans are currently fully or highly protected, highlighting the urgent need for stronger conservation efforts and greater public accountability.

Recognizing the important role journalism plays in informing the public and holding governments accountable, the Earth Journalism Network has launched this grant programme to support high-quality reporting, investigative journalism, newsroom collaboration, and journalist training focused on marine conservation and ocean governance.

Purpose of the Grant

The programme aims to strengthen media coverage of marine conservation by supporting projects that:

  • Produce high-quality investigative and in-depth journalism.
  • Improve public understanding of marine biodiversity.
  • Increase awareness of countries’ commitments toward the 30×30 Target.
  • Hold governments and institutions accountable for marine conservation commitments.
  • Strengthen the capacity of journalists covering environmental and ocean-related issues.
  • Encourage collaboration among media organizations.
  • Promote innovative approaches to environmental journalism.

Ultimately, the programme seeks to improve both the quantity and quality of reporting on marine conservation while encouraging greater public engagement on environmental issues.

Grant Benefits

Successful applicants will receive numerous benefits, including:

Financial Support

Each selected organization may receive funding of up to US$10,000.

EJN expects to award:

  • One grant to Ghana
  • One grant to Mexico
  • One grant to the Philippines
  • Two additional grants to applicants from any other eligible coastal country

Projects with smaller, cost-effective budgets are encouraged, although larger budgets may be considered for particularly innovative and resource-intensive projects.

What Types of Projects Can Be Funded?

The grant supports a broad range of journalism and media development initiatives.

Examples include:

1. Investigative Journalism

Applicants may propose:

  • Multi-part investigative series
  • Long-form reporting
  • Documentary journalism
  • Podcast series
  • Data-driven journalism
  • Cross-border reporting collaborations

Applications proposing fewer than 10 published stories are generally considered less competitive unless they also include journalist training or other capacity-building activities.

2. Journalist Training

Organizations may organize:

  • Workshops
  • Field reporting trips
  • Skills development programmes
  • Ocean journalism training
  • Environmental reporting courses

3. Digital Journalism Resources

Projects may also include:

  • Online learning resources
  • Journalism toolkits
  • Reporting guides
  • Fact-checking tools
  • Interactive mapping projects
  • Data visualization platforms

4. Network Building

Applicants may propose activities that:

  • Strengthen journalist networks
  • Encourage newsroom collaboration
  • Build partnerships between journalists and researchers
  • Foster collaboration with policymakers and information providers

5. Audience Engagement

Projects designed to understand and improve audience engagement are also eligible, including:

  • Audience research
  • Community listening initiatives
  • Public engagement campaigns supporting quality journalism

Priority Reporting Themes

The Earth Journalism Network is especially interested in projects covering:

Government Progress on the 30×30 Target

Reporting may examine:

  • National commitments
  • Policy implementation
  • Conservation regulations
  • Government spending
  • Marine protection strategies

Marine Protected Areas (MPAs)

Possible reporting topics include:

  • Effectiveness of protected areas
  • Enforcement challenges
  • Illegal, unreported and unregulated fishing
  • Marine conservation outcomes

Global Industries Affecting Oceans

Projects may investigate:

  • Industrial fishing
  • International fishing fleets
  • Shipping activities
  • Oil and gas exploration
  • Marine pollution

Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities

Applicants are encouraged to explore:

  • Community participation
  • Indigenous rights
  • Traditional marine management
  • Protected area governance

Technology in Marine Conservation

Potential topics include:

  • Satellite monitoring
  • Artificial intelligence
  • Remote sensing
  • Environmental DNA (eDNA)

International Marine Policy

Projects may focus on:

  • International negotiations
  • Marine finance
  • Biodiversity agreements
  • Ocean governance

High Seas Conservation

Applicants may report on conservation efforts beyond national jurisdictions.

Climate Change

Possible reporting areas include:

  • Coral bleaching
  • Ocean warming
  • Species migration
  • Climate impacts on marine protected areas

Eligibility Requirements

Eligible applicants include media organizations located in coastal countries with marine borders.

Examples include:

  • Newspapers
  • Television broadcasters
  • Radio stations
  • Online media organizations
  • Multimedia journalism platforms
  • Journalist collectives
  • Journalism support organizations
  • Media networks

Applications from Ghana, Mexico, and the Philippines are strongly encouraged.

The following organizations are not eligible:

  • NGOs
  • Civil society organizations
  • Universities
  • Academic institutions
  • Environmental advocacy groups
  • Political campaign organizations

Former EJN grantees may apply, although previous performance will be considered.

Organizations currently receiving another EJN grant are not eligible.

Language Requirements

Applications will only be accepted in:

  • English
  • Spanish

Projects may publish their journalism in any language most appropriate for their audiences.

Budget Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • A detailed project budget
  • Budget justification
  • Completed Excel budget template

Grant funds may be used for:

  • Reporting expenses
  • Journalist training
  • Travel
  • Equipment purchases (where justified)
  • Safety measures
  • Other reasonable project costs

Equipment purchases should represent only a small portion of the total budget and clearly contribute to project outcomes.

Applicants are also welcome to secure additional co-financing, although this is not mandatory.

Please use EJN’s safety tipsheet or another resource of your choice to ensure you are prepared, should you be selected for this grant program. If needed, you should include any security-related costs in your budget.

Project Timeline

Successful applicants can expect the following schedule:

  • Application deadline: 10 August 2026
  • Notification of successful applicants: September 2026
  • Project commencement: October 2026
  • Project completion deadline: 15 September 2027

Applicants should ensure that their proposed activities fit within this implementation period.

Application Assessment Criteria

Applications will be reviewed by a panel of international environmental journalism experts.

Projects will be assessed based on:

  1. Overall quality of the proposal
  2. Relevance to programme objectives
  3. Potential impact
  4. Innovation
  5. Financial feasibility
  6. Organizational capacity
  7. Geographic diversity

Although AI tools may assist with administrative processing, all final funding decisions will be made by human reviewers.

How to Apply

Applicants should follow these steps:

  1. Visit the Earth Journalism Network application portal.
  2. Log in to an existing account or create a new account.
  3. Complete the online application form.
  4. Upload the required supporting documents.
  5. Submit the completed Excel budget template.
  6. Review the application carefully before final submission.

Applicants may save their applications as drafts and return later before submitting.

APPLY NOW

For technical questions, applicants should contact: info.ejn@internews.org

Click HERE to download call for application in Spanish.

Click HERE for tips for detailed instructions on how to create an account, and HERE for detailed instructions on how to reset your password.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: 10 August 2026 (11:59 PM Asia/Manila)

Late applications are unlikely to be considered, so applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their proposals well before the deadline.

Conclusion

The Earth Journalism Network Media Grants 2026–2027 present an excellent opportunity for newsrooms and media organizations committed to strengthening environmental journalism and marine conservation reporting. By providing up to US$10,000 in funding, the programme empowers journalists to produce impactful investigations, train fellow reporters, build collaborative networks, and enhance public understanding of the global effort to protect 30% of the world’s oceans by 2030.

Organizations working in eligible coastal countries are encouraged to develop innovative, high-impact proposals that contribute to stronger accountability, informed public discourse, and meaningful action toward marine conservation. Interested applicants should begin preparing their proposals and supporting documents early to ensure submission before the 10 August 2026 deadline.

For more Grant opportunities, please visit OFY HERE

The post Earth Journalism Network (EJN) Media Grants 2026–2027: Receive Up to US$10,000 to Strengthen Marine Conservation Journalism appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027: Up to ₹14 Lakh Research Funding for India-Japan Collaborative Social Science Projects
item.dc:creator: Bob Waudo
item.description:
The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR), India, in partnership with the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS), Japan, has officially announced the Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027. This prestigious bilateral research funding initiative aims to strengthen academic cooperation between India and Japan by supporting high-quality collaborative research projects and […]

The post ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027: Up to ₹14 Lakh Research Funding for India-Japan Collaborative Social Science Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR), India, in partnership with the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS), Japan, has officially announced the Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027. This prestigious bilateral research funding initiative aims to strengthen academic cooperation between India and Japan by supporting high-quality collaborative research projects and joint seminars in the fields of Social Sciences and Humanities.

The program is designed to build sustainable international research networks, promote knowledge exchange between leading scholars, encourage the participation of early-career researchers, and address pressing social and economic challenges through collaborative research.

Successful Indian research teams can receive funding of up to ₹14,00,000 over two years for Joint Research Projects or up to ₹7,00,000 for Joint Seminars.

The application deadline for Indian applicants is 3 September 2026.

About the ICSSR-JSPS Joint Research Program

The ICSSR-JSPS Joint Research Program is a bilateral academic cooperation initiative established under an agreement between the Indian Council of Social Science Research and the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science.

The initiative seeks to foster long-term collaboration between researchers from India and Japan through:

  • Joint Research Projects
  • Joint Academic Seminars
  • Research networking
  • Exchange of expertise
  • Development of young researchers
  • International academic partnerships

Rather than supporting isolated research activities, the program focuses on building sustainable collaborations capable of generating long-term academic and societal impact.

Objectives of the Program

The program has several important objectives, including:

  • Promoting collaborative research between Indian and Japanese scholars.
  • Strengthening academic cooperation between universities and research institutions.
  • Supporting innovative social science and humanities research.
  • Encouraging interdisciplinary approaches to solving social problems.
  • Facilitating international exchange of knowledge and research expertise.
  • Developing long-term international research networks.
  • Supporting the participation and professional growth of young researchers.
  • Producing research that contributes to policy development and socioeconomic advancement.

Types of Research Activities Supported

The FY2027 call supports two categories of collaborative activities.

1. Joint Research Projects

These projects involve collaborative research conducted by teams from India and Japan.

Key features include:

  • Duration of two years
  • Maximum of 3 projects expected to be funded
  • Projects begin on 1 April 2027
  • Funding of up to ₹7,00,000 per fiscal year
  • Maximum total funding of ₹14,00,000 per project

The research must fall within:

  • Social Sciences
  • Humanities

2. Joint Seminars

The program also funds collaborative seminars involving researchers from India and Japan.

Features include:

  • Duration of up to one week
  • Maximum of 2 seminars
  • Seminars must take place between 1 April 2027 and 31 March 2028
  • Funding up to ₹7,00,000 per seminar

Seminars may be hosted in either:

  • India
  • Japan

Research Fields

The call is specifically intended for research in:

  • Social Sciences
  • Humanities

Researchers working across interdisciplinary themes within these fields are encouraged to apply.

Who Can Apply?

Indian applicants must satisfy several eligibility requirements.

Applicants must:

  • Hold a permanent research or academic position.
  • Be employed at a recognized institution.
  • Hold a PhD or equivalent qualification.
  • Demonstrate strong research capability.
  • Have an established publication record or academic achievements.
  • Be interested in conducting high-quality collaborative international research.

Eligible Indian institutions include:

  • Publicly funded universities
  • Publicly funded colleges
  • UGC-recognized deemed universities
  • Public research institutes
  • ICSSR research institutes
  • ICSSR-recognized research institutions

Visit HERE to learn more about the ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027.

Eligibility for Japanese Researchers

Japanese participants must belong to:

  • Universities
  • Research institutes

operating under the jurisdiction of the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS).

Participation Rules

For Joint Research Projects:

  • No limit on the number of participants.
  • Researchers from third countries are not permitted.

For Joint Seminars:

  • No restriction on participant numbers.
  • Researchers from third countries may participate only if they cover their own expenses.

Funding Available

Joint Research Projects

ICSSR provides financial support for Indian research teams.

Supported expenses include:

International Travel

  • Airfare between India and Japan

Maintenance allowances are provided by JSPS.

Domestic Travel

Support includes:

  • Travel expenses for Indian researchers
  • Domestic travel costs for Japanese researchers while in India
  • Maintenance allowances for Japanese researchers in India

Other Research Costs

Funding may cover:

  • Consumables
  • Printing
  • Communication
  • Honoraria
  • Meetings
  • Administrative research expenses

ICSSR recommends that more than 50% of the project budget be allocated toward international and domestic travel.

Joint Seminars in India

Funding includes:

  • Domestic travel
  • Meeting expenses
  • Communication
  • Printing
  • Reception
  • Excursions
  • Honoraria
  • Seminar logistics

Japanese researchers’ domestic expenses and maintenance allowances are covered during their stay in India.

Joint Seminars in Japan

ICSSR funds:

  • International airfare
  • Domestic travel within India for preparation meetings
  • Communication expenses
  • Printing
  • Preparatory meetings

Seminar organization costs in Japan are covered by the Japanese counterpart.

Application Process

Indian researchers must submit their applications to ICSSR.

Japanese researchers must separately submit matching applications to JSPS.

Applications submitted by only one side will not be considered.

Applicants should therefore coordinate carefully with their international research partners before submission.

APPLY HERE

Application Requirements

Applicants should complete the prescribed application forms provided by ICSSR.

Required documents include:

  • Completed application form
  • Research proposal
  • Institutional affiliation details
  • Supporting documents required under ICSSR guidelines

Researchers previously funded under ICSSR international programs during the past five years must also explain:

  • Results achieved
  • Expected outcomes
  • Relevance to the new proposal

Selection Criteria

Applications undergo rigorous evaluation.

Assessment criteria include:

  1. Scientific excellence.
  2. Quality of research proposal.
  3. Importance of international collaboration.
  4. Knowledge transfer potential.
  5. Contribution to solving social challenges.
  6. Socioeconomic impact.
  7. Participation of young researchers.
  8. Feasibility of implementation.
  9. Research methodology.
  10. Policy relevance.
  11. Budget appropriateness.
  12. Suitability of seminar venue (where applicable).

Review committees from ICSSR and JSPS may conduct separate evaluations before jointly selecting successful proposals.

Obligations of Selected Researchers

Successful Indian team leaders must:

  • Submit implementation plans.
  • Request project funding using prescribed forms.
  • Administer funds through their affiliated institutions.
  • Submit final reports.
  • Publicly acknowledge ICSSR and JSPS support in publications.
  • Complete research ethics education (for research projects).

Ethical Responsibilities

ICSSR maintains strict standards regarding research integrity.

Funding may be terminated if researchers engage in:

  • Fabrication
  • Falsification
  • Plagiarism
  • Misuse of funds
  • Harassment
  • Discrimination
  • Violations of applicable laws

Researchers are expected to maintain the highest ethical standards throughout the project.

Intellectual Property

Project leaders from both countries must ensure:

  • Proper protection of intellectual property
  • Fair distribution of research outputs
  • Compliance with national regulations

Institutions are encouraged to establish appropriate intellectual property policies before the project begins.

Important Notes

Applicants should keep the following in mind:

  • Matching applications are mandatory from both India and Japan.
  • Projects start from 1 April 2027.
  • Selection numbers may change depending on available budgets.
  • ICSSR does not arrange visas or accommodation.
  • ICSSR is not responsible for accidents or medical emergencies during project-related travel.
  • Reports submitted may be published on the ICSSR website.

Application Deadline

Deadline for Indian researchers: 3 September 2026

Late applications are not expected to be accepted. Applicants are encouraged to coordinate with their Japanese counterparts well in advance to ensure both submissions are completed before the deadline.

Conclusion

The ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027 represents an outstanding opportunity for experienced researchers in India and Japan to establish meaningful international collaborations in the social sciences and humanities. Beyond financial support, the initiative promotes long-term academic partnerships, interdisciplinary research, knowledge exchange, and the development of early-career researchers. Institutions and scholars seeking to address important societal challenges through collaborative research should take advantage of this prestigious bilateral funding opportunity by preparing strong proposals and ensuring coordinated submissions from both partner countries before the application deadline of 3 September 2026.

For more global grant opportunity visit OFY HERE

The post ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027: Up to ₹14 Lakh Research Funding for India-Japan Collaborative Social Science Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Coordinator for ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme (2026): Remote Opportunity to Lead Europe’s Next Generation of Cancer Prevention Advocates
item.dc:creator: Dora Kumi
item.description:
The Association of European Cancer Leagues (ECL) is inviting applications for the position of Coordinator for the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme. This exciting part-time opportunity is designed for passionate professionals who are interested in cancer prevention, youth engagement, leadership, and international collaboration. The successful candidate will play a central role in coordinating one of Europe’s […]

The post Coordinator for ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme (2026): Remote Opportunity to Lead Europe’s Next Generation of Cancer Prevention Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Association of European Cancer Leagues (ECL) is inviting applications for the position of Coordinator for the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme. This exciting part-time opportunity is designed for passionate professionals who are interested in cancer prevention, youth engagement, leadership, and international collaboration.

The successful candidate will play a central role in coordinating one of Europe’s leading youth advocacy initiatives focused on cancer prevention. Working closely with the ECL Secretariat and Board, the Coordinator will oversee the implementation of the programme’s strategic goals while supporting young advocates from across Europe. This role offers flexible remote working arrangements, competitive hourly compensation, and the opportunity to contribute to a meaningful public health mission that has positively impacted young people from more than 30 European countries.

About the Association of European Cancer Leagues (ECL)

The Association of European Cancer Leagues (ECL) is a respected non-profit umbrella organization representing 35 national and regional cancer leagues across Europe.

The organization works to improve cancer prevention, treatment, patient care, and public health policies throughout Europe.

Its vision is straightforward but ambitious:

A Europe free of cancer.

ECL members include trusted non-profit organizations that provide:

  • Cancer prevention education
  • Patient support services
  • Public awareness campaigns
  • Advocacy for stronger cancer policies
  • Information and healthcare resources

ECL also maintains strict ethical standards by refusing financial support from the tobacco industry and other companies whose products are considered harmful to health.

About the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme

Launched in 2015, the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme has become one of Europe’s leading youth-led cancer prevention initiatives. The programme brings together enthusiastic young people committed to improving public health through awareness, education, advocacy, and policy engagement.

Its primary educational framework is the European Code Against Cancer, which serves as a practical guide for reducing cancer risks through healthy lifestyle choices and preventive measures.

Since its establishment, the programme has successfully built a network of youth advocates from more than 30 European countries.

Participants come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds including:

  • Medicine
  • Public Health
  • Political Science
  • Law
  • Health Policy
  • Social Sciences
  • Research
  • Other health-related disciplines

Throughout the year, Youth Ambassadors participate in:

  • Public awareness campaigns
  • International events
  • Educational workshops
  • Online advocacy initiatives
  • Summer Schools
  • Networking activities
  • Policy discussions
  • Community engagement projects

The programme empowers young leaders to promote cancer prevention at both national and European Union levels.

About the Coordinator Position

ECL is seeking a part-time Youth Ambassadors Programme Coordinator who will provide operational leadership and strategic coordination for the programme.

The selected individual will work closely with:

  • The ECL Secretariat
  • The ECL Board
  • The Senior Policy Officer for Cancer Prevention
  • Youth Ambassadors across Europe

The Coordinator will ensure that the programme continues to grow while successfully implementing its strategic objectives and annual action plans.

Although the initial contract lasts for one year, ECL intends to establish a longer-term working relationship with the successful candidate, subject to performance and future collaboration opportunities.

Key Responsibilities

The Coordinator will perform a wide range of leadership, planning, and administrative responsibilities.

These include:

1. Programme Coordination

The Coordinator will oversee the day-to-day management of the Youth Ambassadors Programme.

Responsibilities include:

  • Coordinating programme activities
  • Supporting Youth Ambassadors
  • Maintaining effective communication with members
  • Facilitating collaboration among participants

 

2. Strategic Leadership

The successful candidate will help implement the programme’s long-term strategy by:

  • Drafting Action Plans
  • Implementing strategic objectives
  • Monitoring programme progress
  • Reporting on programme achievements
  • Evaluating outcomes and future priorities

 

3. Funding Support

The Coordinator will work with the Secretariat to identify and support funding opportunities that strengthen the programme’s sustainability.

Tasks may include:

  • Researching funding opportunities
  • Assisting with grant-related activities
  • Supporting project development

 

4. Event Organisation

The Coordinator will organize major programme activities including:

  • Annual Summer School
  • Youth Ambassador meetings
  • Workshops
  • Online events
  • Additional programme activities when required.

Who Should Apply?

ECL is searching for an organized, motivated, and reliable individual with a passion for youth leadership and cancer prevention.

Applicants should demonstrate the ability to work independently while collaborating with an international team.

Priority consideration will be given to current or former members of the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme, although applications from all qualified candidates are encouraged.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should possess the following qualifications:

  • A university degree
  • Familiarity with the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme
  • Strong organizational skills
  • Excellent written and spoken English
  • High levels of trustworthiness and reliability
  • Experience in or strong interest in cancer prevention
  • Ability to work in an international and multicultural environment

 

Benefits of the Position

Successful applicants will receive several professional benefits, including:

  • One-year part-time contract
  • Possibility of contract extension
  • Flexible remote working arrangements
  • Opportunity to work from Brussels, Belgium
  • Competitive hourly rate of €30
  • Flexible workload of approximately 10–20 hours per month
  • Opportunity to collaborate with leading European public health professionals
  • Valuable international networking experience
  • Leadership experience within a major European non-profit organization
  • Contribution to cancer prevention initiatives across Europe

Work Location

Applicants may choose between:

  • Remote work
  • Brussels, Belgium

The flexible work arrangement allows candidates from different locations to contribute effectively while maintaining work-life balance.

Contract Details

  • Contract Duration: One year
  • Extension: Possible based on future collaboration
  • Employment Type: Part-time freelance consultancy
  • Expected Start Date: As soon as possible
  • Workload: Approximately 10–20 hours per month
  • Hourly Rate: €30

How to Apply

Interested applicants should prepare the following documents:

  • Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Two professional references
  • Letter of motivation

Applications should be submitted via email: vacancy@cancer.eu

Email Subject Line: Application – YA coordinator

The deadline for submitting applications is 22 July 2026.

Learn more about this job HERE

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible because:

  • Interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis.
  • Shortlisted candidates will be contacted within one week after the application deadline.
  • Due to the anticipated high volume of applications, ECL will not provide individual feedback to unsuccessful applicants.       Why You Should Consider This Opportunity

This position offers much more than a part-time consultancy role. It provides an opportunity to contribute directly to one of Europe’s most impactful youth health advocacy programmes.

Professionals interested in public health, cancer prevention, youth empowerment, international cooperation, and policy advocacy will gain valuable experience working with experts and young leaders from across Europe.

The role also offers flexibility, international exposure, leadership experience, and the chance to make a meaningful difference in cancer prevention efforts that benefit communities across Europe.

Whether you have previously participated in the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme or are an experienced professional with a passion for public health, this position provides an excellent platform for advancing your career while contributing to an important cause.

Conclusion

The Coordinator for the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme is an outstanding opportunity for individuals who are passionate about youth leadership, public health, and cancer prevention. With flexible remote working options, competitive compensation, and the opportunity to coordinate an influential international programme, this role is ideal for professionals seeking meaningful impact within the European public health sector. Interested candidates should ensure they submit a complete application before the 22 July 2026 deadline to maximize their chances of consideration.

For more Global JOBS click HERE

The post Coordinator for ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme (2026): Remote Opportunity to Lead Europe’s Next Generation of Cancer Prevention Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN Innovation Training Library 2026: Explore Courses, Toolkits and Future-Ready Professional Skills
item.dc:creator: Muhammad Chishti
item.description:
The United Nations Innovation Network provides an extensive Innovation Training Library containing courses, toolkits, learning paths, professional certificates and other capacity-building resources developed or hosted by organizations across the United Nations system. The library is intended to help United Nations personnel and other interested learners build practical innovation capabilities. It allows users to explore new […]

The post UN Innovation Training Library 2026: Explore Courses, Toolkits and Future-Ready Professional Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Innovation Network provides an extensive Innovation Training Library containing courses, toolkits, learning paths, professional certificates and other capacity-building resources developed or hosted by organizations across the United Nations system.

The library is intended to help United Nations personnel and other interested learners build practical innovation capabilities. It allows users to explore new approaches, understand emerging technologies and consider how innovative methods can be applied within United Nations operations, public institutions, development programmes and other professional environments.

Rather than operating as one course with a single application form, deadline or eligibility requirement, the UN Innovation Training Library functions as a searchable directory. Each listed resource has its own subject, duration, delivery format, intended audience and access arrangements.

The library covers areas such as:

  • Artificial intelligence and machine learning
  • Generative artificial intelligence
  • Data innovation and analytics
  • Digital transformation
  • Behavioural science
  • Strategic foresight
  • Human-centred design
  • Innovation management
  • Innovation scaling
  • Sustainable and innovative finance
  • Blockchain
  • Automation and robotics
  • Internet of Things
  • Communications and knowledge sharing
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Climate action
  • Digital health
  • Digital agriculture
  • Tourism innovation
  • Urban development
  • Aviation innovation
  • Frontier technologies

Important Clarification About the Number of Resources

The training page currently displays a gallery counter showing 99 results. However, this number represents the total results appearing in the library at the time the page is viewed.

It should not be interpreted as 99 conventional courses because the collection also contains:

  • Toolkits
  • Learning paths
  • Professional certificate programmes
  • Training compilations
  • Fellowship programmes
  • Accelerator programmes
  • Mobile microlearning resources
  • Other professional development materials

For this reason, it is more accurate to describe the platform as a growing collection of innovation-related courses, toolkits and learning resources rather than stating that it provides a fixed number of courses.

The library is also described as a living library, meaning that its content and total number of results can change when new resources are added or existing resources are revised or removed.

What Is the UN Innovation Training Library?

The UN Innovation Training Library is a curated collection of learning resources from across the United Nations system.

Its purpose is to support the development of innovation capabilities among UN staff and other interested users. The official page states that the library can support both people who are new to innovation and professionals who are already leading teams or innovation-related activities.

The library can be used by learners seeking to:

  1. Understand the fundamentals of innovation.
  2. Develop an innovation-oriented professional mindset.
  3. Learn how artificial intelligence can support organizational work.
  4. Understand the ethical and governance implications of AI.
  5. Improve data literacy, analysis and visualization.
  6. Use evidence more effectively in policymaking.
  7. Explore digital transformation in government and development.
  8. Apply behavioural science to public policy and programme design.
  9. Use human-centred design to improve products and services.
  10. Learn about sustainable finance and climate-related investment.
  11. Study emerging technologies such as blockchain and robotics.
  12. Improve training, facilitation and communication skills.
  13. Explore innovation in agriculture, health, tourism, aviation and cities.
  14. Connect professional learning with the Sustainable Development Goals.

How the Training Library Is Organized

The page contains filters that allow users to search according to development priorities, training providers, innovation subjects, time requirements and delivery methods.

1. Sustainable Development Goal Filters

Users can search for resources related to humanitarian action or the Sustainable Development Goals.

The available filters include:

  • Humanitarian Action
  • SDG 1: No Poverty
  • SDG 2: Zero Hunger
  • SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being
  • SDG 4: Quality Education
  • SDG 5: Gender Equality
  • SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation
  • SDG 7: Affordable and Clean Energy
  • SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
  • SDG 9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • SDG 10: Reduced Inequalities
  • SDG 11: Sustainable Cities and Communities
  • SDG 12: Responsible Consumption and Production
  • SDG 13: Climate Action
  • SDG 14: Life Below Water
  • SDG 15: Life on Land
  • SDG 16: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
  • SDG 17: Partnerships for the Goals

These filters allow learners to connect their professional-development choices with specific development, humanitarian, environmental or governance priorities.

2. Training Provider Filters

The library contains resources associated with numerous United Nations entities, specialized agencies and international institutions.

Examples appearing in the provider filter include:

  • Food and Agriculture Organization
  • International Civil Aviation Organization
  • International Labour Organization
  • International Telecommunication Union
  • United Nations Capital Development Fund
  • United Nations Conference on Trade and Development
  • United Nations Development Programme
  • United Nations Environment Programme
  • United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change
  • United Nations Global Pulse
  • United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees
  • United Nations Human Settlements Programme
  • United Nations Institute for Training and Research
  • United Nations International Computing Centre
  • United Nations System Staff College
  • United Nations Tourism
  • United Nations University
  • UN Women
  • UNICEF
  • World Food Programme
  • World Health Organization
  • World Bank Group
  • World Intellectual Property Organization
  • World Meteorological Organization

The complete filter contains many additional United Nations departments, offices and partner organizations.

3. Innovation Subject Filters

Users can search the library according to the following innovation-related themes:

  • 3D printing
  • Artificial intelligence and machine learning
  • Automation and robotics
  • Behavioural science
  • Blockchain
  • Chatbots
  • Communications and knowledge sharing
  • Culture of innovation
  • Data innovation
  • Digital transformation
  • Drones and satellite imagery
  • Frontier technologies
  • Generative AI
  • Innovation in business processes
  • Innovation in general, including human-centred design
  • Innovation scaling
  • Innovative finance
  • Internet of Things
  • Mobile phone-based innovations
  • New business models
  • Strategic foresight
  • Virtual reality and augmented reality

These categories demonstrate that the library addresses both technology-driven innovation and wider institutional or organizational change.

4. Time Requirements

The library divides resources into four broad time categories:

  • Short: A few hours
  • Medium: A few days
  • Long: A few weeks
  • Very long: A few months

The duration assigned to each listing is an indicative category rather than a detailed schedule. Users should check the individual programme page for the actual number of hours, days or weeks required.

5. Delivery Methods

The available delivery modalities are:

  • Online
  • In-person
  • Hybrid

Most displayed resources are online, but the library also includes classroom-based and blended programmes.

Major Learning Areas Available in the Library

Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning

Artificial intelligence is one of the most prominent subject areas represented in the library.

The available resources cover different levels of AI learning, including:

  • Basic AI literacy
  • Generative AI
  • Large language models
  • Prompt engineering
  • AI governance
  • AI ethics
  • AI applications in health
  • AI applications in hospitality
  • AI for learning professionals
  • AI for decision-makers
  • AI and data storytelling
  • AI skills for UN personnel

AI Academy

The AI Academy was developed and is operated by the UNICC AI Hub.

It seeks to address the artificial-intelligence literacy gap across United Nations entities by offering a structured, multi-partner initiative aligned with UN 2.0 and the World Food Programme’s Data and AI Competency Framework.

The library classifies it as a medium-duration online resource.

Artificial Intelligence for Learning Professionals

This UNITAR course examines how AI can be integrated into learning design without sacrificing empathy, human judgement and professional insight.

It combines areas such as:

  • Behavioural science
  • Instructional design
  • Strategic foresight
  • Ethical AI
  • Human-centred learning

The programme is presented as a short online course.

Artificial Intelligence Masterclass

The Artificial Intelligence Masterclass explores the influence of AI on:

  • Training
  • Education
  • Knowledge management
  • Workforce development
  • Everyday professional practices

The course also examines emerging trends, ethical considerations and practical applications. Its description identifies students, educators and professionals as potential learners.

AI Skills for UN Personnel

AI Skills for UN Personnel is led by the United Nations System Staff College in collaboration with the Executive Office of the Secretary-General and Microsoft Elevate.

It supports the UN 2.0 and UN80 agendas and is classified as a medium-duration hybrid programme.

AI Essentials for Decision Makers

This UNITAR programme is intended to help institutional leaders, government professionals and organizational decision-makers understand AI and lead digital transformation responsibly.

It connects technical concepts with practical policy and management applications, including:

  • Governance
  • Strategic foresight
  • Organizational performance
  • Responsible digital transformation

The library identifies it as a long online programme.

UN Generative AI Professional Certificate

The UN Generative AI Professional Certificate provides learning on the mechanisms and applications of generative AI in:

  • Text
  • Images
  • Video

It is listed as a long hybrid programme rather than a multi-month programme.

Generative AI With Large Language Models

This course provides more technical learning about the lifecycle of large language model applications.

It addresses:

  • Data gathering
  • Model selection
  • Transformer architecture
  • Model training
  • Fine-tuning
  • Performance evaluation
  • Deployment
  • Scaling laws
  • Compute requirements
  • Inference
  • Business opportunities and challenges

It is categorized as a medium-duration online course.

AI Governance, Ethics and Responsible Technology

The library does not present AI only as a technical subject. Several listings focus on responsible governance and ethical use.

These include:

  • Artificial Intelligence Governance Toolkit
  • AI Governance in Practice: Developing Secure and Innovative Frameworks
  • Generative AI and Ethics: The Urgency of Now
